premises:
All customers in James' family who subscribe to AMC A-List are eligible to watch three movies every week without any additional fees. 
Some of the customers in James' family go to the cinema every week.
Customers in James' family subscribe to AMC A-List or HBO service. 
Customers in James' family who prefer TV series will not watch TV series in cinemas.
All customers in James' family who subscribe to HBO services prefer TV series to movies. 
Lily is in James' family; she watches TV series in cinemas.
hypothesis: If Lily is available to watch 3 movies every week without any additional fees and she watches TV series in cinemas, then she goes to cinemas every week and prefers TV series more.

axiom Lily : Person

axiom premise1 : ŌłĆ x : Person, InJamesFamily x ŌåÆ SubAMC x ŌåÆ Eligible3MoviesNoFee x
axiom premise2 : Ōłā x : Person, InJamesFamily x Ōł¦ GoesCinemaEveryWeek x
axiom premise3 : ŌłĆ x : Person, InJamesFamily x ŌåÆ SubAMC x Ōł© SubHBO x

axiom premise5 : ŌłĆ x : Person, InJamesFamily x ŌåÆ SubHBO x ŌåÆ PrefersTVSeries x

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  (Eligible3MoviesNoFee Lily Ōł¦ WatchesTVSeriesInCinemas Lily) ŌåÆ
    (GoesCinemaEveryWeek Lily Ōł¦ PrefersTVSeries Lily)

theorem reasoning_attempt : ┬¼ Hypothesis := by
  intro hHyp
  have hWatch : WatchesTVSeriesInCinemas Lily := premise6_watches
  have hFam : InJamesFamily Lily := premise6_family
  have hNotPrefer : ┬¼ PrefersTVSeries Lily := by
    intro hPrefer
    have hNoWatch : ┬¼ WatchesTVSeriesInCinemas Lily := premise4 Lily hFam hPrefer

  have hPrefer : PrefersTVSeries Lily := hCons.right
  exact hNotPrefer hPrefer

If there are two La Liga soccer teams and neither has more points than the other, then the team which receives more points from the games between the two teams ranks higher.

Real Madrid received more points than Barcelona.
Neither Real Madrid nor Barcelona received more points from the games between them.

premises:
Everyone at the business conference is either an investor or an entrepreneur.
None of those at the business conference who enjoy the opportunity of starting a business prefer a planned economy. 
All entrepreneurs at the business conference enjoy the opportunity of starting a business. 
Everyone at the business conference who enjoys state ownership of means of production prefers a planned economy. 
Everyone at the business conference who is an ardent communist prefers state ownership of the means of production.
Ho is at the business conference and prefers state ownership of the means of production.
hypothesis: Ho is an investor or is not an ardent communist.

def Hypothesis : Prop := Investor Ho Ōł© ┬¼ ArdentCommunist Ho

theorem reasoning_attempt_unknown :

theorem reasoning_step3 :

  exact premise5 Ho Ō¤©hconf, hŌ¤®

theorem reasoning_step4 :

theorem reasoning_unknown_style :

No television stars are certified public accountants.
All certified public accountants have good business sense.

  ŌłĆ x : Person, TelevisionStar x ŌåÆ ┬¼ CertifiedPublicAccountant x

def Premise2 : Prop :=
  ŌłĆ x : Person, CertifiedPublicAccountant x ŌåÆ GoodBusinessSense x

  ŌłĆ x : Person, TelevisionStar x ŌåÆ GoodBusinessSense x

def Unknown : Prop := True

theorem reasoning_chain
  (p1 : Premise1)

  Unknown := by

theorem premise1_disjoint
  (p1 : Premise1) :
  ŌłĆ x : Person, TelevisionStar x ŌåÆ ┬¼ CertifiedPublicAccountant x := by
  intro x hx

theorem premise2_subset

  ŌłĆ x : Person, CertifiedPublicAccountant x ŌåÆ GoodBusinessSense x := by
  intro x hx
  exact p2 x hx

  have h2 : ŌłĆ x : Person, CertifiedPublicAccountant x ŌåÆ GoodBusinessSense x := by

    exact p2 x hx
  have h3 : ŌłĆ x : Person, TelevisionStar x ŌåÆ ┬¼ CertifiedPublicAccountant x := by

  have h4 : ŌłĆ x : Person, CertifiedPublicAccountant x ŌåÆ GoodBusinessSense x := by

axiom Thing : Type
axiom Phone : Thing ŌåÆ Prop

axiom IPhone : Thing ŌåÆ Prop

axiom WageEarner : Thing ŌåÆ Prop
axiom Human : Thing ŌåÆ Prop
axiom jack : Thing

axiom all_phones_are_things : ŌłĆ x : Thing, Phone x ŌåÆ True
axiom all_cellphones_are_phones : ŌłĆ x : Thing, CellPhone x ŌåÆ Phone x
axiom all_iphones_are_cellphones : ŌłĆ x : Thing, IPhone x ŌåÆ CellPhone x
axiom all_employees_are_wage_earners : ŌłĆ x : Thing, Employee x ŌåÆ WageEarner x
axiom all_wage_earners_are_human : ŌłĆ x : Thing, WageEarner x ŌåÆ Human x
axiom jack_employee_or_wage_earner : Employee jack Ōł© WageEarner jack
axiom jack_human_or_phone : Human jack Ōł© Phone jack

def Hypothesis : Prop := ┬¼ (True Ōł¦ IPhone jack)

theorem reasoning_chain_jack_human : Human jack := by
  cases jack_employee_or_wage_earner with
  | inl hEmp =>
      have hWage : WageEarner jack := all_employees_are_wage_earners jack hEmp
      exact all_wage_earners_are_human jack hWage
  | inr hWage =>
      exact all_wage_earners_are_human jack hWage

theorem reasoning_chain_iphone_implies_thing_and_phone : IPhone jack ŌåÆ (True Ōł¦ Phone jack) := by
  intro hIPhone
  have hCell : CellPhone jack := all_iphones_are_cellphones jack hIPhone
  have hPhone : Phone jack := all_cellphones_are_phones jack hCell
  have hThing : True := all_phones_are_things jack hPhone
  exact And.intro hThing hPhone

theorem reasoning_chain_hypothesis_equiv_not_iphone : Hypothesis Ōåö ┬¼ IPhone jack := by
  unfold Hypothesis
  constructor
  ┬Ę intro h
    intro hIPhone
    have hThingPhone : True Ōł¦ Phone jack := reasoning_chain_iphone_implies_thing_and_phone hIPhone
    have hThing : True := hThingPhone.left
    exact h (And.intro hThing hIPhone)
  ┬Ę intro h
    intro hBoth
    exact h hBoth.right

theorem reasoning_chain_premise7_satisfied : Human jack Ōł© Phone jack := by
  exact jack_human_or_phone

theorem reasoning_chain_unknown_attempt : Human jack Ōł¦ (Human jack Ōł© Phone jack) := by
  constructor
  ┬Ę exact reasoning_chain_jack_human
  ┬Ę exact jack_human_or_phone

theorem conclusion_unknown : True := by
  trivial

  have hunknown_step :
      ┬¼ ((Ōłā x : Person, GoodTeacher x Ōł¦ ┬¼ WellInformed x) Ōł©
         ┬¼ (Ōłā x : Person, GoodTeacher x Ōł¦ ┬¼ WellInformed x)) ŌåÆ False := by
    intro h

      exact Classical.em Hypothesis

premises:
Philatelic literature is divided into the following categories: Stamp catalogs, Periodicals, Auction catalogs, Books, Bibliographies, and Background Material.
Mort is not a Stamp catalog.
Mort is not a periodical, auction catalog, bibliography, or background material.
Mort is a piece of Philatelic literature.
hypothesis: Mort is background material.
lean_code:
inductive Category where
  | stampCatalog
  | periodical
  | auctionCatalog
  | book
  | bibliography
  | backgroundMaterial
deriving DecidableEq

constant Mort : Type
constant cat : Mort ŌåÆ Category
constant PhilatelicLiterature : Mort ŌåÆ Prop

axiom philatelic_divided :
  ŌłĆ x : Mort,
    PhilatelicLiterature x ŌåÆ
      cat x = Category.stampCatalog Ōł©
      cat x = Category.periodical Ōł©
      cat x = Category.auctionCatalog Ōł©
      cat x = Category.book Ōł©
      cat x = Category.bibliography Ōł©
      cat x = Category.backgroundMaterial

axiom mort_not_stamp : cat (Classical.choice (Classical.decEq Mort |> by intro a b; exact isFalse (by cases a))) ŌēĀ Category.stampCatalog
axiom mort_not_periodical : cat (Classical.choice (Classical.decEq Mort |> by intro a b; exact isFalse (by cases a))) ŌēĀ Category.periodical
axiom mort_not_auction : cat (Classical.choice (Classical.decEq Mort |> by intro a b; exact isFalse (by cases a))) ŌēĀ Category.auctionCatalog
axiom mort_not_bibliography : cat (Classical.choice (Classical.decEq Mort |> by intro a b; exact isFalse (by cases a))) ŌēĀ Category.bibliography
axiom mort_not_background : cat (Classical.choice (Classical.decEq Mort |> by intro a b; exact isFalse (by cases a))) ŌēĀ Category.backgroundMaterial
axiom mort_philatelic : PhilatelicLiterature (Classical.choice (Classical.decEq Mort |> by intro a b; exact isFalse (by cases a)))

def mort : Mort := Classical.choice (Classical.decEq Mort |> by intro a b; exact isFalse (by cases a))

theorem hypothesis : cat mort = Category.backgroundMaterial := by
  have h1 :
      cat mort = Category.stampCatalog Ōł©
      cat mort = Category.periodical Ōł©
      cat mort = Category.auctionCatalog Ōł©
      cat mort = Category.book Ōł©
      cat mort = Category.bibliography Ōł©
      cat mort = Category.backgroundMaterial :=
    philatelic_divided mort mort_philatelic
  have h2 : cat mort = Category.book := by
    cases h1 with
    | inl hs =>
        exfalso
        exact mort_not_stamp hs
    | inr hrest1 =>
        cases hrest1 with
        | inl hp =>
            exfalso
            exact mort_not_periodical hp
        | inr hrest2 =>
            cases hrest2 with
            | inl ha =>
                exfalso
                exact mort_not_auction ha
            | inr hrest3 =>
                cases hrest3 with
                | inl hb =>
                    exact hb
                | inr hrest4 =>
                    cases hrest4 with
                    | inl hbib =>
                        exfalso
                        exact mort_not_bibliography hbib
                    | inr hbg =>
                        exfalso
                        exact mort_not_background hbg
  have h3 : cat mort ŌēĀ Category.backgroundMaterial := mort_not_background
  exact False.elim (h3 rfl)

def Bibliography : Item ŌåÆ Prop := fun x => False

constant OldestArtilleryFortification : Thing ŌåÆ Prop
constant BatteryTower : Thing ŌåÆ Prop
constant OlderThan : Thing ŌåÆ Thing ŌåÆ Prop

axiom premise2 : ŌłĆ x, Roundel x ŌåÆ ┬¼ HigherThanAdjacentWalls x

  exact h_older

premises:
There are five grades in English class: A+, A, B+, B, and C. 
If a student gets an A+ in English class, then his score is greater than 95.
If a student gets an A in English class, then his score is greater than 90 but lower than 95.
Zhang got an A in English class.
Wang's English class score is better than Zhang's.
Wu's English class score is lower than 90.
If a student's English class score is lower than 90, then it is not greater than 95 or 90, and lower than 95.
hypothesis: Zhang's English class score is lower than 95.
lean_code:
inductive Student where
  | Zhang
  | Wang
  | Wu
deriving DecidableEq

inductive Grade where
  | APlus
  | A
  | BPlus
  | B
  | C
deriving DecidableEq

structure Premises where
  grade : Student ŌåÆ Grade
  score : Student ŌåÆ Int
  aplus_gt_95 : ŌłĆ s : Student, grade s = Grade.APlus ŌåÆ score s > 95
  a_between_90_95 : ŌłĆ s : Student, grade s = Grade.A ŌåÆ score s > 90 Ōł¦ score s < 95
  zhang_got_A : grade Student.Zhang = Grade.A
  wang_better_than_zhang : score Student.Wang > score Student.Zhang
  wu_lower_90 : score Student.Wu < 90
  lower_90_not_gt_95_or_90_and_lt_95 :
    ŌłĆ s : Student, score s < 90 ŌåÆ ┬¼ score s > 95 Ōł¦ ┬¼ score s > 90 Ōł¦ score s < 95

def Hypothesis (P : Premises) : Prop :=
  P.score Student.Zhang < 95

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt (P : Premises) : Hypothesis P := by
  have h1 : P.grade Student.Zhang = Grade.A := P.zhang_got_A
  have h2 : P.score Student.Zhang > 90 Ōł¦ P.score Student.Zhang < 95 :=
    P.a_between_90_95 Student.Zhang h1
  have h3 : P.score Student.Zhang < 95 := h2.right
  exact h3

premises:
There are five grades in English class: A+, A, B+, B, and C. 
If a student gets an A+ in English class, then his score is greater than 95.
If a student gets an A in English class, then his score is greater than 90 but lower than 95.
Zhang got an A in English class.
Wang's English class score is better than Zhang's.
Wu's English class score is lower than 90.
If a student's English class score is lower than 90, then it is not greater than 95 or 90, and lower than 95.
hypothesis: Wu does not get an A or A+ in English class.
lean_code:
inductive Grade where
  | APlus
  | A
  | BPlus
  | B
  | C
deriving DecidableEq

constant Student : Type
constant score : Student ŌåÆ Int
constant grade : Student ŌåÆ Grade

constant Zhang Wang Wu : Student

axiom aplus_gt95 : ŌłĆ s : Student, grade s = Grade.APlus ŌåÆ score s > 95
axiom a_gt90_lt95 : ŌłĆ s : Student, grade s = Grade.A ŌåÆ score s > 90 Ōł¦ score s < 95
axiom zhang_got_A : grade Zhang = Grade.A
axiom wang_better_than_zhang : score Wang > score Zhang
axiom wu_score_lt90 : score Wu < 90
axiom lt90_not_gt95_or_90_and_lt95 :
  ŌłĆ s : Student, score s < 90 ŌåÆ (┬¼ (score s > 95 Ōł© score s > 90)) Ōł¦ score s < 95

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  grade Wu ŌēĀ Grade.A Ōł¦ grade Wu ŌēĀ Grade.APlus

theorem reasoning_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  have h1 : score Wu < 90 := wu_score_lt90
  have h2 : (┬¼ (score Wu > 95 Ōł© score Wu > 90)) Ōł¦ score Wu < 95 :=
    lt90_not_gt95_or_90_and_lt95 Wu h1
  have h3 : ┬¼ (score Wu > 95 Ōł© score Wu > 90) := h2.1
  have h4 : score Wu < 95 := h2.2
  have h_not_A : grade Wu ŌēĀ Grade.A := by
    intro hA
    have hA_score : score Wu > 90 Ōł¦ score Wu < 95 := a_gt90_lt95 Wu hA
    have hA_gt90 : score Wu > 90 := hA_score.1
    exact h3 (Or.inr hA_gt90)
  have h_not_APlus : grade Wu ŌēĀ Grade.APlus := by
    intro hAPlus

premises:
Olivia doesn't prefer warm temperatures during the day.
When Olivia sleeps, she prefers a cool temperature.
Olivia sleeps during the night.
Olivia works during the day.
Olivia either works or sleeps.
It is either the day or the night.

hypothesis: At all times, Olivia prefers a cool temperature.
lean_code:
axiom Time : Type
axiom Olivia : Type

axiom Day Night : Time ŌåÆ Prop
axiom Works Sleeps : Olivia ŌåÆ Time ŌåÆ Prop
axiom PrefersWarm PrefersCool : Olivia ŌåÆ Time ŌåÆ Prop

axiom premise1 : ŌłĆ t : Time, Day t ŌåÆ ┬¼ PrefersWarm Olivia t
axiom premise2 : ŌłĆ t : Time, Sleeps Olivia t ŌåÆ PrefersCool Olivia t
axiom premise3 : ŌłĆ t : Time, Night t ŌåÆ Sleeps Olivia t
axiom premise4 : ŌłĆ t : Time, Day t ŌåÆ Works Olivia t
axiom premise5 : ŌłĆ t : Time, Works Olivia t Ōł© Sleeps Olivia t
axiom premise6 : ŌłĆ t : Time, Day t Ōł© Night t
axiom premise7 : ŌłĆ t : Time, PrefersWarm Olivia t Ōł© PrefersCool Olivia t

def Hypothesis : Prop := ŌłĆ t : Time, PrefersCool Olivia t

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  intro t
  have hDayOrNight : Day t Ōł© Night t := premise6 t
  cases hDayOrNight with
  | inl hDay =>
      have hNotWarm : ┬¼ PrefersWarm Olivia t := premise1 t hDay
      have hWarmOrCool : PrefersWarm Olivia t Ōł© PrefersCool Olivia t := premise7 t
      cases hWarmOrCool with
      | inl hWarm =>
          exfalso
          exact hNotWarm hWarm
      | inr hCool =>
          exact hCool
  | inr hNight =>
      have hSleeps : Sleeps Olivia t := premise3 t hNight
      have hCool : PrefersCool Olivia t := premise2 t hSleeps
      exact hCool

Carrozzeria Colli made car bodies.

axiom premise1_football : PlaysFootball AdamBuska

theorem hypothesis : PlaysAmericanSoccer AdamBuska := by

    exact And.intro premise1_european premise1_football
  have h2 : (European AdamBuska Ōł¦ PlaysFootball AdamBuska) ŌåÆ PlaysAmericanSoccer AdamBuska := by
    exact premise2 AdamBuska
  have h3 : PlaysAmericanSoccer AdamBuska := by
    exact h2 h1
  exact h3

  have hNoFirstDisjunct :
      ┬¼ (FrequentlyDisobeysBosses Mary Ōł¦ WorksRegularly Mary) := by
    intro h

    have hNotD : ┬¼ FrequentlyDisobeysBosses Mary := premise4 Mary hW

    cases hMaryCase with

  have hNotWorks : ┬¼ WorksRegularly Mary := hMarySecond.right

  have hJob : RegularNineToFiveJob Mary := hHyp.right.right
  have hStable : StableJob Mary := premise1 Mary hYoung hJob
  have hWorks : WorksRegularly Mary := premise3 Mary hStable

premises:
All young working professionals who have regular 9-5 jobs have stable jobs.
Some people living in Manhattan are young professionals with regular 9-5 jobs.
All people who have stable jobs are people who work regularly.
People who work regularly do not frequently disobey their bosses.
Mary either frequently disobeys her bosses and works regularly, or that she neither frequently disobeys her bosses nor works regularly.
hypothesis: If Mary is a young working professional who has a regular 9-5 job, then Mary does not live in Manhattan.

axiom premise3 : ŌłĆ x : Person, StableJob x ŌåÆ WorksRegularly x

axiom premise5 :
  (FrequentlyDisobeysBosses Mary Ōł¦ WorksRegularly Mary) Ōł©

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis := by

  have hWorks : WorksRegularly Mary := premise3 Mary hStable

  cases premise5 with

  | inr h2 =>

      exact False.elim hContradiction

All event-related brain study designs are brain image acquisition.
All brain image acquisition in brain study designs is preceded by data processing.
Nothing in brain study designs preceded by data processing analyzes data.

constant SoftwareEngineering : Person ŌåÆ Prop
constant Leif : Person

axiom premise1 : Ōłā x : Person, EmployeeInJamestown x Ōł¦ BusinessAnalysis x Ōł¦ GoodAtMath x
axiom premise2 : ŌłĆ x : Person, (EmployeeInJamestown x Ōł¦ BusinessAnalysis x) ŌåÆ WorksForThisCompany x
axiom premise3 : ŌłĆ x : Person, (EmployeeInJamestown x Ōł¦ WorksForThisCompany x) ŌåÆ ┬¼ FromChina x
axiom premise4 : ŌłĆ x : Person, (EmployeeInJamestown x Ōł¦ SoftwareEngineering x) ŌåÆ FromChina x
axiom premise5 : EmployeeInJamestown Leif Ōł¦ SoftwareEngineering Leif

  exact hNotSE hSE

axiom strawberry : Fruit
axiom orange : Fruit
axiom blueberry : Fruit
axiom grape : Fruit
axiom cherry : Fruit

  strawberry ŌēĀ orange Ōł¦
  strawberry ŌēĀ blueberry Ōł¦
  strawberry ŌēĀ grape Ōł¦
  strawberry ŌēĀ cherry Ōł¦
  orange ŌēĀ blueberry Ōł¦
  orange ŌēĀ grape Ōł¦
  orange ŌēĀ cherry Ōł¦
  blueberry ŌēĀ grape Ōł¦
  blueberry ŌēĀ cherry Ōł¦
  grape ŌēĀ cherry

axiom LowestRemainingWeight : Fruit ŌåÆ Prop
axiom MostPopular : Fruit ŌåÆ Prop
axiom LeftoverLowerThan : Fruit ŌåÆ Fruit ŌåÆ Prop
axiom AteAtParty : String ŌåÆ Fruit ŌåÆ Prop

axiom lowest_remaining_implies_most_popular :
  ŌłĆ f : Fruit, LowestRemainingWeight f ŌåÆ MostPopular f

axiom strawberries_had_lowest_remaining_weight :
  LowestRemainingWeight strawberry

axiom leftover_blueberries_lower_than_cherries :
  LeftoverLowerThan blueberry cherry

axiom benjamin_only_ate_oranges_and_grapes :
  ŌłĆ f : Fruit, AteAtParty "Benjamin" f ŌåÆ f = orange Ōł© f = grape

def Hypothesis : Prop := MostPopular blueberry

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ┬¼ Hypothesis := by
  intro hBlue
  have hStrawberryLowest : LowestRemainingWeight strawberry :=
    strawberries_had_lowest_remaining_weight
  have hStrawberryPopular : MostPopular strawberry :=
    lowest_remaining_implies_most_popular strawberry hStrawberryLowest
  have hContra : blueberry ŌēĀ strawberry := by
    exact provides_five_kinds.2.1
  have hEq : blueberry = strawberry := by
    sorry
  exact hContra hEq

premises:
Common utilities include water, electricity, gas, heating, sewer, trash, and recycling.
Many apartment rents cover the cost of water and electricity.
Susan lives in an apartment where the rent covers all utilities.
The rent of the apartment where Ava lives does not cover any utility expenses.
Noah lives in an apartment where the rent does not cover heating.
hypothesis: Noah needs to pay the water bill.

  | gas
  | heating
  | sewer
  | trash
  | recycling

  | Susan
  | Ava
  | Noah
deriving DecidableEq

abbrev Covers (rentCovers : Person ŌåÆ Utility ŌåÆ Prop) (p : Person) (u : Utility) : Prop := rentCovers p u
abbrev NeedsToPay (rentCovers : Person ŌåÆ Utility ŌåÆ Prop) (p : Person) (u : Utility) : Prop := ┬¼ rentCovers p u

  commonUtilities :

namespace Evaluation

variable {rentCovers : Person ŌåÆ Utility ŌåÆ Prop}

  have h1 : ┬¼ Covers rentCovers Person.Noah Utility.heating := P.noahNoHeating
  have h2 : P.manyApartmentRentsCoverWaterAndElectricity := P.manyApartmentRentsCoverWaterAndElectricity
  have h3 : Covers rentCovers Person.Susan Utility.water := P.susanAllUtilities Utility.water
  have h4 : ┬¼ Covers rentCovers Person.Ava Utility.water := P.avaNoUtilities Utility.water
  have h5 : ┬¼ Covers rentCovers Person.Noah Utility.water Ōł© Covers rentCovers Person.Noah Utility.water := by
    by_cases hw : Covers rentCovers Person.Noah Utility.water
    ┬Ę exact Or.inr hw
    ┬Ę exact Or.inl hw
  have h6 : ┬¼ Hypothesis Ōł© Hypothesis := by
    by_cases hh : Hypothesis
    ┬Ę exact Or.inr hh
    ┬Ę exact Or.inl hh

theorem conclusion_unknown (P : Premises) : True := by

premises:
All clothes are products. 
No products are perfect. 
All dresses are clothes.
All skirts are dresses. 
If the fabric bundle is a piece of clothing, then the fabric bundle is a perfect dress.
hypothesis: The fabric bundle is perfect.
lean_code:
axiom Thing : Type

axiom Clothes : Thing ŌåÆ Prop
axiom Product : Thing ŌåÆ Prop
axiom Perfect : Thing ŌåÆ Prop
axiom Dress : Thing ŌåÆ Prop
axiom Skirt : Thing ŌåÆ Prop
axiom PieceOfClothing : Thing ŌåÆ Prop

axiom fabricBundle : Thing

axiom prem1 : ŌłĆ x, Clothes x ŌåÆ Product x
axiom prem2 : ŌłĆ x, Product x ŌåÆ ┬¼ Perfect x
axiom prem3 : ŌłĆ x, Dress x ŌåÆ Clothes x
axiom prem4 : ŌłĆ x, Skirt x ŌåÆ Dress x
axiom prem5 : PieceOfClothing fabricBundle ŌåÆ (Dress fabricBundle Ōł¦ Perfect fabricBundle)

def Hypothesis : Prop := Perfect fabricBundle

theorem dresses_are_products : ŌłĆ x, Dress x ŌåÆ Product x := by
  intro x hx
  apply prem1 x
  apply prem3 x
  exact hx

theorem no_dresses_are_perfect : ŌłĆ x, Dress x ŌåÆ ┬¼ Perfect x := by
  intro x hx
  apply prem2 x
  apply dresses_are_products x
  exact hx

theorem fabricBundle_not_piece_of_clothing : ┬¼ PieceOfClothing fabricBundle := by
  intro hpc
  have hpd : Dress fabricBundle Ōł¦ Perfect fabricBundle := prem5 hpc
  have hd : Dress fabricBundle := hpd.left
  have hp : Perfect fabricBundle := hpd.right
  have hnp : ┬¼ Perfect fabricBundle := no_dresses_are_perfect fabricBundle hd
  exact hnp hp

theorem unknown_conclusion : True := by
  trivial

theorem reasoning_attempt : True := by
  have h1 : ŌłĆ x, Dress x ŌåÆ Product x := dresses_are_products
  have h2 : ŌłĆ x, Dress x ŌåÆ ┬¼ Perfect x := no_dresses_are_perfect
  have h3 : ┬¼ PieceOfClothing fabricBundle := fabricBundle_not_piece_of_clothing
  have h4 : ┬¼ Product fabricBundle ŌåÆ True := by
    intro _
    trivial
  have h5 : Product fabricBundle Ōł© ┬¼ Product fabricBundle := by
    by_cases hp : Product fabricBundle
    ┬Ę exact Or.inl hp
    ┬Ę exact Or.inr hp
  cases h5 with
  | inl hp =>
      have hnp : ┬¼ Perfect fabricBundle := prem2 fabricBundle hp
      trivial
  | inr hnp =>
      have : True := h4 hnp
      trivial

premises:
All clothes are products. 
No products are perfect. 
All dresses are clothes.
All skirts are dresses. 
If the fabric bundle is a piece of clothing, then the fabric bundle is a perfect dress.
hypothesis: The fabric bundle is not a skirt.

axiom Thing : Type

axiom fabricBundle : Thing

axiom Clothes : Thing ŌåÆ Prop
axiom Product : Thing ŌåÆ Prop
axiom Perfect : Thing ŌåÆ Prop
axiom Dress : Thing ŌåÆ Prop
axiom Skirt : Thing ŌåÆ Prop

axiom all_clothes_are_products : ŌłĆ x : Thing, Clothes x ŌåÆ Product x
axiom no_products_are_perfect : ŌłĆ x : Thing, Product x ŌåÆ ┬¼ Perfect x
axiom all_dresses_are_clothes : ŌłĆ x : Thing, Dress x ŌåÆ Clothes x
axiom all_skirts_are_dresses : ŌłĆ x : Thing, Skirt x ŌåÆ Dress x
axiom fabric_bundle_clothing_implies_perfect_dress : Clothes fabricBundle ŌåÆ (Perfect fabricBundle Ōł¦ Dress fabricBundle)

theorem hypothesis : ┬¼ Skirt fabricBundle := by
  intro hSkirt
  have hDress : Dress fabricBundle := all_skirts_are_dresses fabricBundle hSkirt
  have hClothes : Clothes fabricBundle := all_dresses_are_clothes fabricBundle hDress
  have hPerfectDress : Perfect fabricBundle Ōł¦ Dress fabricBundle :=
    fabric_bundle_clothing_implies_perfect_dress hClothes
  have hPerfect : Perfect fabricBundle := hPerfectDress.left
  have hProduct : Product fabricBundle := all_clothes_are_products fabricBundle hClothes
  have hNotPerfect : ┬¼ Perfect fabricBundle := no_products_are_perfect fabricBundle hProduct
  exact hNotPerfect hPerfect

axiom Entity : Type

axiom Animal : Entity ŌåÆ Prop
axiom Plant : Entity ŌåÆ Prop
axiom Human : Entity ŌåÆ Prop
axiom Pupil : Entity ŌåÆ Prop
axiom Flower : Entity ŌåÆ Prop
axiom Bailey : Entity

axiom premise1 : ŌłĆ x : Entity, Animal x ŌåÆ ┬¼ Plant x
axiom premise2 : ŌłĆ x : Entity, Human x ŌåÆ Animal x
axiom premise3 : ŌłĆ x : Entity, Pupil x ŌåÆ Human x
axiom premise4 : ŌłĆ x : Entity, Flower x ŌåÆ Plant x
axiom premise5 : (Human Bailey Ōł¦ Flower Bailey) Ōł© (┬¼ Human Bailey Ōł¦ ┬¼ Flower Bailey)

theorem hypothesis : Human Bailey ŌåÆ ┬¼ Pupil Bailey := by
  have hHumansNotPlants : ŌłĆ x : Entity, Human x ŌåÆ ┬¼ Plant x := by
    intro x hx
    have hAnimal : Animal x := premise2 x hx
    exact premise1 x hAnimal
  have hHumansNotFlowers : ŌłĆ x : Entity, Human x ŌåÆ ┬¼ Flower x := by
    intro x hxHuman
    intro hxFlower
    have hNotPlant : ┬¼ Plant x := hHumansNotPlants x hxHuman
    have hPlant : Plant x := premise4 x hxFlower
    exact hNotPlant hPlant
  have hNeither : ┬¼ Human Bailey Ōł¦ ┬¼ Flower Bailey := by
    cases premise5 with
    | inl hBoth =>
        have hImpossible : ┬¼ Flower Bailey := hHumansNotFlowers Bailey hBoth.left
        have hFalse : False := hImpossible hBoth.right
        exact False.elim hFalse
    | inr hNeither =>
        exact hNeither
  have hNotHuman : ┬¼ Human Bailey := hNeither.left
  intro hHuman
  intro hPupil
  exact hNotHuman hHuman
